key_points: Integers include positive numbers, negative numbers, and zero
Adding two positive integers results in a positive integer
Adding two negative integers results in a negative integer
The sum of an integer and its additive inverse is always zero
On a number line, adding a positive integer means moving to the right
examples: Additive Inverse: The additive inverse of $7$ is $-7$
exam_tips: Remember that 0 is neither positive nor negative
Always keep the common sign when adding integers of the same type
Visualize the number line if you get confused with directions
Subtraction of a positive is the same as adding a negative
